g.data-narration: Welcome to Cloud Security and Privacy Impact Assessment. Let's explore why cloud security is the top concern for organizations.
g.text: Cloud Security: Risks and Privacy Impact Assessment

g.data-narration: Cloud security ranks as the top concern because organizations relinquish direct physical control over their data and must trust third-party providers.
g.text: Must trust provider with sensitive data

g.data-narration: Cloud security encompasses practices, technologies, and controls protecting cloud environments from unauthorized access, use, and destruction.
The shared responsibility model divides security duties: providers secure infrastructure while customers protect their data, access, and applications.
Data breaches are the most feared risk - unauthorized access from misconfigured storage or compromised credentials. Data loss means permanent destruction.
Account hijacking occurs through phishing or weak passwords. Insecure APIs provide entry points since every cloud service exposes APIs.
Malicious insiders abuse authorized access. DoS attacks overwhelm cloud resources, making services unavailable to legitimate users.
g.text: Provider or customer staff misusing access

g.data-narration: Shared technology vulnerabilities allow cross-tenant access in multi-tenant environments. Insufficient due diligence exposes organizations to unanticipated risks.
g.text: VM escape, hypervisor vulnerabilities in multi-tenant

g.data-narration: The Cloud Security Alliance's Treachable Twelve lists the top twelve cloud security threats that organizations must understand and mitigate.
The remaining six threats include APTs, data loss, due diligence failures, cloud service abuse, DoS, and shared technology issues.
A Privacy Impact Assessment is a systematic process evaluating how systems collect, use, share, and protect personal information to identify risks.
g.text: What is a Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA)?
Systematic process evaluating data collection, use,
Why PIA is Critical for Cloud

g.data-narration: PIA involves identifying need, describing information flow, and identifying privacy risks - evaluating threats to personal data.
g.text: Analyze threats - unauthorized access, excessive collection

g.data-narration: Assess and mitigate risks, then document and review. PIAs should integrate with development lifecycle, not be an afterthought.
g.text: Create formal PIA report, update as needed
Part of SDLC, not an afterthought

g.data-narration: Key frameworks include GDPR requiring DPIAs for high-risk processing, HIPAA for health data, SOC 2 for provider audits, and ISO 27001 standards.
Remember: Cloud security is top concern due to loss of control. Know CSA threats, shared responsibility model, and PIA process for exams.

g.text: Cloud security is #1 concern due to relinquishing direct physical control
Major risks: Data breaches, account hijacking, insecure APIs, malicious insiders
Shared responsibility model: Provider secures infrastructure, customer protects data
